title: Missing validation of position timestamp against current time
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/adapter.c:515-530

set_position_fix_time validates timestamps against a minimum epoch floor:
  if (unix_time < position->effective_epoch_floor) {
      position->fix_time_rejected_below_epoch_floor = true;
      ...
  }

But there's no validation against an upper bound (future timestamps). A malicious or buggy peer could send position data with timestamps far in the future (e.g., year 2100), which would be accepted.

Consider adding:
1. Maximum reasonable timestamp validation
2. Skew detection relative to local time (if available)

This affects SECURITY: accepting arbitrary future timestamps could cause cache/ordering issues downstream.
close_reason: Implemented future timestamp validation using CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_POSITION_MAX_FUTURE_SKEW in set_position_fix_time; added flag to struct; resolved incidental Kconfig merge conflict
